name: design-rules
description: Company-wide visual strategy principles for Web2App funnels. Component implementation details belong to runtime components, renderer registry, and theme contracts.

rules.description: Every funnel should derive its visual direction from the actual product, audience, movement style, intensity, and emotional promise. Do not default to a generic health-app look.

Before choosing a palette, analyze age band, gender focus, intensity, movement style, confidence level, and emotional promise. Fitness/health/wellness category alone is not a valid reason to choose green, teal, pink, black, military colors, or any other palette.

The primary action color must come from a named source: App Store visual evidence, product brand assets, explicit user direction, or a deliberate strategy fallback. Do not invent saturated colors as generic defaults.
When screenshots are provided as layout or interaction references, do not copy their brand colors unless the user explicitly asks for those colors.

Use one quiet app canvas background that supports the product without competing with content. The background may be white, dark neutral, off-white, or a subtle product-tinted neutral selected by strategy.
The funnel should feel like a focused app onboarding experience, not a generic marketing landing page. The entry portal may be immersive, but the rest of the flow should remain task-focused.
Desktop should render as a real Web2App page with a centered content column and full-width top context, not as a decorative phone mockup.
The entry_page portal should use a dominant hero image, brand/product promise, start-funnel button, and login button. It should feel like a direct app portal rather than a multi-section landing page.
Avoid decorative gradient blobs, bokeh orbs, abstract SVG waves, fake glassmorphism, generic AI dashboard decoration, and nested card-on-card compositions.
Typography should fit mobile widths, avoid awkward wrapping, use letter spacing 0, and keep hierarchy appropriate to the page density.
Paywalls should look like real mobile subscription screens connected to the generated plan, not SaaS pricing tables.
Login, account overview, subscription management, and cancellation pages should use the same theme direction, typography, and layout language as the rest of the funnel.
